title: Xtra Crunchy Peruvian Potatoes Papa Rellena
description: A malty, crunchy, romantic Peruvian dish featuring ice cream and nuka-pickle, this low-glycemic-friendly papa rellena is a unique dessert, with a crunchy outside and a creamy inside, perfect for a special occasion, the combination of flavors and textures will make it a memorable experience
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Wash and peel the potatoes, then cut them into large chunks.
Boil the potatoes in salted water until they are slightly tender.
Drain the potatoes and let them cool.
In a bowl, mix together the chopped nuka-pickle, malt powder, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Add the cooled potatoes to the bowl and mix until they are well coated with the nuka-pickle mixture.
Divide the potato mixture into 4 equal parts and shape each part into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and make a hole in the center of each disk.
Fill each hole with a scoop of ice cream and top with a sprinkle of grated cheese.
Fold the potato disk over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the papa rellena with coconut oil and bake for 20 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
